 {"id":"ft-f4u9x","title":"docs: cli-reference still advertises ft query alias","description":"docs/cli-reference.md and docs/distributed-security-spec.md still claim top-level `ft query` exists as an alias for `ft search`.

Current binary evidence on 2026-04-23:
- `./FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft --help` lists `search` but no `query`
- `./FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft query --help` resolves to `ft search` help text rather than a distinct top-level command surface

Docs should stop describing `ft query` as a first-class CLI command/alias and instead document `ft search` as the canonical surface.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:02:24.062588Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:08:48.727667Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:08:48.726764Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 64d9be31: cli docs now document ft search as the canonical top-level search command and stop implying ft query is a first-class root command.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-f6vhg","title":"[ft-7iof6.5] delete no-op asupersync-runtime feature flag + every required-features reference","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:21:27.082223Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T02:37:49.398236Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T02:37:49.398068Z","close_reason":"Verification under this bead falsified the premise. The Cargo.toml comment at line 677 ('no-op: asupersync is now always-on') is misleading — the feature is NOT a no-op for tests/benches: it gates 50 test files via #![cfg(feature = ...)], 10 bench files, 16 required-features declarations, and several PAIRED #[cfg(not(...))] legacy-tokio bodies (runtime_compat_tests.rs, robot_sdk_transport_integration.rs, proptest_runtime_compat.rs, replay_labruntime.rs, web.rs, mux_pool_scaling.rs, tailer.rs). Trial deletion produced compile errors: tailer/sync_primitives benches lost their main fn (file-level gate evaluated to false dropping the module), tailer body's no-feature tx.reserve() broke against asupersync's reserve(&cx) signature. d79490d7 lands the smallest correct change: replace the 'no-op' comment with accurate documentation of the feature's role (test/bench selector preserving paired legacy-tokio probes for migration-conformance) and point at the follow-up bead. Filed ft-nm5nc (parent-child of ft-f6vhg) with the full retirement sequence: audit cfg(not(...)) bodies → drop file-level gates → THEN delete feature + required-features + default entry. cargo check --all-targets passes via rch on /tmp/ft-cc6-target.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","cleanup","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-f6vhg","depends_on_id":"ft-7iof6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:21:32.229049Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-fbee5","title":"pattern pack discovery followed symlinks out of tree","description":"# [HIGH] pattern-pack loader: symlink-escape via discover_packs_from_dir

**Subsystem:** patterns / scan_pipeline
**File:** crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs

## The hole

`load_pack_from_file(path, root)` routes through `sandbox_resolve` only when a
`Some(root)` is supplied. `discover_packs_from_dir`, used for
`.ft/patterns/**` discovery from a workspace root, was calling
`load_pack_from_file(path, None)` for every file + rules.toml it iterated —
so the sandbox check was **skipped** even though the dir itself was
workspace-attacker-controlled.

A hostile repo could ship:

    .ft/patterns/config.yaml -> /Users/victim/.ssh/id_rsa

`path.is_file()` follows the symlink (returns true), `load_pack_from_file`
opens the target, feeds its contents to `serde_yaml::from_str`, and on parse
failure emits:

    tracing::warn!("Skipping invalid user pack {}: {e}", path.display());

Which can echo excerpts of the target file via serde's error reporting
(`mapping expected, found: …`).

## Pre-conditions

* victim clones a hostile repo that contains a symlink under
  `.ft/patterns/`;
* frankenterm loads user packs at session start.

## Fix

`discover_packs_from_dir` now passes `Some(dir)` as the sandbox root to
`load_pack_from_file`. `sandbox_resolve` canonicalizes and rejects any
resolved path outside `dir`. Added regression test
`discover_packs_rejects_symlink_escape`.

## Residual risk

A top-level symlink replacing `.ft/patterns` itself would still escape
because the caller (`load_packs_from_config`) builds `ws_dir =
root_path.join(".ft").join("patterns")` without canonicalizing. That's a
follow-up — tracking separately.

## Sibling work

- 33e84436 security(patterns): add sandbox_resolve helper (helper introduction)
- e8397747 / 8e57c8e0 fix(limits): MAX_PACK_BYTES 16 MiB cap (orthogonal DoS layer)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:26:10.019601Z","created_by":"p3-review-audit","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:54:22.244142Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:54:22.243799Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 6ab52765 — canonicalize + in-tree check applied to pack discovery","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ft-05hfm","patterns","security"]}
-{"id":"ft-fdppm","title":"[patterns] rate-limit regexes can pair distant anchors with unrelated retry text","description":"Reasoning mode: statistical. The engine uses anchors only to select candidate rules, then applies a rule regex across the entire text segment. Builtin Codex and Claude rate-limit rules have anchors such as rate limit/429/529, but regexes that only look for retry/reset/wait/back off plus a duration (patterns.rs:1573-1588 and 1719-1731). A segment containing one rate-limit phrase and an unrelated 'wait 5 minutes' elsewhere can emit a false rate_limit.detected. Fix: require regex evidence to overlap or be near the matched anchor, or make these regexes include the rate-limit anchor terms; add a regression covering distant unrelated retry text.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:39:40.538033Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:08:21.200266Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-fdppm","title":"[patterns] rate-limit regexes can pair distant anchors with unrelated retry text","description":"Reasoning mode: statistical. The engine uses anchors only to select candidate rules, then applies a rule regex across the entire text segment. Builtin Codex and Claude rate-limit rules have anchors such as rate limit/429/529, but regexes that only look for retry/reset/wait/back off plus a duration (patterns.rs:1573-1588 and 1719-1731). A segment containing one rate-limit phrase and an unrelated 'wait 5 minutes' elsewhere can emit a false rate_limit.detected. Fix: require regex evidence to overlap or be near the matched anchor, or make these regexes include the rate-limit anchor terms; add a regression covering distant unrelated retry text.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:39:40.538033Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:19:48.104862Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:19:48.104383Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-fjelf","title":"[MEDIUM] MCP envelopes leak raw storage/runtime strings via McpToolError::from_error (crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_error.rs:38-43)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:12:43.691734Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T19:09:15.168474Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T19:09:15.168135Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 51058daf: McpToolError::from_error now redacts storage/runtime details","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","review","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-fk3rn","title":"[patterns] claude_code.model.selected lacks near-miss corpus freeze for broad model anchors","description":"Rule claude_code.model.selected in crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs anchors on broad text like 'model:' and 'Using model' but corpus coverage under crates/frankenterm-core/tests/corpus/claude_code/ only has positive fixtures (model_selected.txt + model_selected_v2.txt). There is no negative near-miss corpus pair proving generic non-Claude 'Using model' or help/config text does not spuriously fire the rule.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:27:26.599498Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:36:50.259953Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:36:50.259599Z","close_reason":"Near-miss corpus landed in f109c706: model_selected_near_miss.{txt,expect.json}. Fixture was pre-staged by another pane; verified its content matches the ft-fk3rn intent before adopting — anchors match on L3 ('Using model: gpt-4o-mini') and L4 (doc quote of 'model:') but the regex at patterns.rs:1919 requires the captured model to start with 'claude-(opus|sonnet|haiku)' so both fail. expect.json is []. Global Python-regex equivalent search returns None. Matches the ft-u4m4z / ft-8ku8p near-miss pattern.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
